title: nyfed / survey consumer expectations / inflation expectations | Economic Commentary | Short-Term Inflation Expectations Increase as Gas Price Grow...
summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York's March 2026 Survey of Consumer Expectations shows median one-year-ahead inflation expectations increased 0.4 percentage point to 3.4%, while three-year-ahead expectations rose to 3.1% and five-year-ahead remained unchanged at 3.0%. Gas price growth expectations surged to 9.4%, the highest level since March 2022. Labor market sentiment showed mixed signals with mean unemployment expectations reaching 43.5% (highest since April 2025), while mean job finding expectations improved to 45.9%.
